title: heliophila obibensis
intro: Heliophila obibensis, also known as the African wormwood or devil's thorn, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat worm infestations, fever, and skin infections due to its antiparasitic and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits include its use in treating conditions such as malaria, dysentery, and snake bites, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Heliophila obibensis is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its essential oil can also be extracted for use in traditional medicine.
